title: Nexus common definitions (v1alpha2)
description: Reusable definitions added by the Personal + Hub product layer. TARGET-STATE: none of these contracts is implemented yet; they define the agreed shape before code. This library ADDS to v1alpha1 (edition/capability/subscription/organization/package-access), it does not break it: crypto/time/identifier primitives are reused from v1alpha1 by absolute $id.

Edition.description: Product edition of an instance. Orthogonal to deployment modality (see v1alpha2/deployment-modality.schema.json). `personal` is the free single-owner OSS edition; `team` and `organization` require a valid Hub entitlement.

CapabilityId.description: Stable, edition-independent capability identifier the Runtime understands (e.g. 'team_collaboration', 'fleet_management', 'premium_pack_access'). NEVER a plan or price name: the Runtime knows capabilities, not commercial plans, so the plan catalogue can change without touching the Runtime.

PackageVisibility.description: Registry distribution lane. `public`/`community` are OSS and installable without a Hub account and MUST be mirrorable. `verified-premium`/`private-organization` require a valid entitlement and a short-lived download grant (never DRM).

PublisherVerification.description: Publisher curation state shown in the catalogue.

SubscriptionState.description: Lifecycle state of a subscription/entitlement. Degradation is graceful: owner access and export are preserved in EVERY state; nothing is deleted or encrypted on expiry.

OrgRole.description: Role of a member inside an organization/team. `owner` is the single instance owner (the Personal-edition actor); additional roles require a Team/Organization entitlement. `readonly` and `guest` never gain write access.

PackageScope.description: Access scope granted over Registry lanes. Free lanes ('public', 'community') need no scope; restricted lanes are named as 'verified-premium:<catalog>' or 'private-organization:<org_id>'.

GracePeriodDays.description: Bounded offline grace window (days) the cached entitlement remains honoured after expiry before graceful degradation. A Hub outage must never take a customer instance down; it degrades per the subscription lifecycle table.

OrganizationId.description: Stable, opaque organization identifier. Absent for Personal edition (single owner, no organization). Lowercase-canonical (`org_` + lowercase alphanumerics) so it round-trips unchanged inside a PackageScope 'private-organization:<org_id>' segment; mixed-case ids are rejected to avoid an id that cannot be expressed as a scope.

UserRef.description: Opaque, non-identifying reference to a Runtime-local user profile. NEVER an email or other PII; the Hub does not receive user content or identity beyond an opaque handle. Lowercase-canonical for consistency with OrganizationId.

OfflineVerification.description: Metadata that lets the embedded Entitlement Verifier check a signature locally, with NO network call. The verifier pins the Hub key by key_id under a trust_domain and applies the canonicalization before verifying.

trust_domain.description: Trust root the verifier resolves the entitlement key against (e.g. 'hub.nexusos.dev').

verifier_key_id.description: Pinned public key id the Runtime uses to verify offline. Matches signature.key_id.

canonicalization.description: Canonicalization applied to the payload before signing/verifying.

network_check_required.description: Personal and cached Team verification NEVER require an online check. Pinned false by construction.
